column3: Subtitle
Welcome to my series,
where I continue to eat frozen ramen kits for breakfast.
I'm in Vancouver to visit my parents.
I have found this local Japanese store
that sold ramen kits,
and the first one you told me to try is this
Japanese curry udon.
Curry udon was a staple
I ate regularly when I was living in Japan.
I love how tender that pork belly was,
but this tofu skin
was a little sweeter than I liked it to be.
The curry soup was just okay.
It was a bit sour.
The udon was sensational.
It was nice and slippery.
But which ramen kit should I try next?",4700000,462800,3024,26300,Zenya,"88 W Pender St, Vancouver, BC V6B 6N9, Canada",1.08634924721,1

column3: Subtitle
Welcome to my series
where I eat frozen ramen kits for breakfast.
I have three days left in Vancouver,
but four more ramen kits,
so I'm trying to finish them off before I leave.
Today I'm trying to spicy miso tonkotsu flavor.
It's from a local ramen dealer here in Vancouver,
and I'm curious how this compares to Rajan
spicy tonkotsu.
I love the chasu.
It's very fatty and juicy,
and the ramen comes with a bunch of veggies.
The veggies are okay,
but I like how they make the ramen healthier.
The broth is flavorful,
but not as flavorful as raijan.
And in noodles,
personally, I wish they were chewier.",2900000,300200,2383,17800,Tonkotsu Ramen Tsukiya,"740 Denman St, Vancouver, BC V6G 2L5, Canada",1.43012006737,2

column3: Subtitle
This is one of the largest soup dumplings
that money can buy.
It's a very traditional dimsum dish,
but there are only a few dimsum masters
left in the world who know how to make it.
So very few dimsum restaurants actually carry it.
But I was lucky
enough to find a restaurant that made it.
What's shocking is the amount of soup
in these soup dumplings.
There's enough soup to fill up a whole bowl.
But that's not even the craziest part.
The craziest part is that these soup dumplings
are loaded with shrimp and scallops.
But the big question is,
how do they taste?
As someone who's eaten dimsum all his life,
this is one of the bougiest dimsum dishes.
It got me laughing with joy.",2100000,198500,1318,11200,Chef's Choice Seafood Restaurant,"955 W Broadway, Vancouver, BC V5Z 1K3, Canada",2.14796395537,3

column1: 0e0de8d1-0964-4b25-9fa9-42c13dede118,a6b63336-f87e-4f48-81c2-533fc0c8d338,84f237a9-d8fc-4b4b-9156-298d7834b5f6,insta.noodls,https://www.tiktok.com/@insta.noodls/video/7332650836146916613,"My parents homecooking (dumplings, seafood, and noodles) @BEAVO did i do it right? Chili crisp by @That Apron Guy ","Start Time
column3: Subtitle
This is everything my parents cook for me
for lunch and dinner at their home in Vancouver.
I'm not home often,
but when I do,
they cook up a storm.
And if you're wondering
why I love soup dumplings so much,
I guess I got it from my parents.
These are my parents go to soup dumplings
and you can get them at TNT, by the way.
I'm in my Beaver era.
I'm trying to swallow everything in one bite.
Won't recommend doing that with soup dumplings.
But my mom also made me my favorite
turnip cakes from scratch.
I also tried to swallow this in one bite
and I'm glad to say I live to see another day.
But dinner was even more epic.
We had seafood hot pot with homemade dumplings.
My parents bought a whole live crab
so you know the seafood is fresh.
And I was eating these dungeons
crab legs like they were lollipops.
We also got some fresh oyster still in its shell.
This is the Cantonese way to eat it with some ginger
scallions and soy sauce.
But my parents saved the best for last,
dipping the noodles in delicious crab head butter
and topping it with homemade dumplings.
This was by far the best bite I had in Vancouver.
Nothing beats fresh seafood
and nothing beats home cooking.",1300000,162000,316,8048,Newton Hotpot,"13631 100 Ave, Surrey, BC V3T 1H9, Canada",22.87883745081,5

column3: Subtitle
Trying to be my dad's favorite son
by buying him the most expensive Cantonese
king crab meal for Father's Day.
Let's see how much it cost me. First,
we had to fly from Toronto to Vancouver
to attend his epic king crab meal
because my parents live in Vancouver.
We bought our favorite snacks to pass the time for the 5 hour flight.
And once we got to Vancouver,
I started practice my Mandarin
because I wanted to impress my dad by ordering a Mandarin.
Do you want it? Do you want it?
Somehow the manager understood me
because we got a massive whole live king crab cook
three ways Cantonese style.
Immediately
you smell the garlic and butter that drenches these king crab legs.
You get two massive plates of king crab legs.
I could tell my dad really liked it.
He already ate four legs.
It's just pure king crab like me drenched in that garlic butter.
But for the king crab body,
they deep fried the massive chunks of meat and garlic and chilies.
This is the most essential classic Cantonese way of preparing seafood.
Absolutely stunning.
But this might be my favorite part of the king crab.
They take the head of the king crab
and stuff it with king crab fried rice
topped with cheese and Portuguese style sauce.
There were loads of king crab in this king crab fried rice.
This was a decadent dish. My dad approved and named me his favorite son.
And it only cost me $668.",1300000,165700,4489,8754,Sun Sui Wah Seafood Restaurant,"3888 Main St, Vancouver, BC V5V 3N9, Canada",3.86561722852,4

The ambience is really great in the restaurant. Absolutely stunning! The service was amazing and we were impressed by how accommodating the restaurant was all things considered!
We ordered:
- Mac & Cheese
- Brussels Sprouts
- Wagyu Trio (A5 Miyazaki, Japan - Jade Pure, Australia - Carrara Farms, Australia)
- Tuna Tartare (complimentary)
- Sticky Toffee Pudding (complimentary)

So as the video describes, part of the wagyu trio was overcooked. The restaurant was so accommodating and fired off a new one for us. In the interim, they also gave us a tuna tartare while we waited. The recooked wagyu trio was cooked to perfection, the perfect medium rare as requested. As expected, each ounce was beautifully marbled and particularly, the A5 melted with each bite.
Our server, Allan, was incredible. Attentive, kind, entertaining. He even gave us a free dessert for our anniversary.

It’s not a good idea to splurge this much unless you have a boatload of money, but for very special occasions, I highly suggest trying this place out at least once! We’ll definitely be back. I need to try that famous beef wellington one day! See ya later 🫶🏾

7d2b3f66-39aa-4a28-9b81-3062fe6d853c,3e5ae551-7cfe-4e9a-a461-2f5059686406,7ca83eab-cc91-487d-a275-27dc40bcc6d8,jaycyj.pov,https://www.tiktok.com/@jaycyj.pov/video/7609905953017842964,"So I checked out Northern Grill. They’re hidden in a lumberyard in Vancouver.

They’ve been voted Canada’s number 1 eats, have won awards for breakfast and brunch and specifically was named in Vancouver Magazine as best brunch. With all these accolades, you guys know I had to try it out!

From the service to the food, I had an incredible experience. The servers were honestly amazing, checking in as needed and were easy to track down. The owner was so friendly, gave us tips on how to elevate the flavors of our food and really made us feel like we were at home. With all of the hype surrounding this place, I honestly need to say they lived up to it.
I got to try:
- pan fried gyoza
- mama’s wontons
- spicy deep fried dumplings (HIGHLY RECOMMEND)
- wonton egg noodle soup (with bbq pork)
- chicken & french toast (HIGHLY RECOMMEND)
- fried onion ring burger (HIGHLY RECOMMEND)
- crispy chicken burger

With over 8000 reviews and averaging 4.9 stars on google, I easily rate the food and my experience a 5/5. I will absolutely be back to try more things; this really was an amazing spot to check out and I hope you all do too!
